desc:
Greatest of Arda's many rivers, the Anduin thunders as it moves quickly from
north to south, bordered on the east by high green banks and ancient woodlands.
In the west, the tumbling waters of a small stream smash into the mighty river
with no effect on the Anduin's deliberate course. To the south, the current
seems to become even more powerful, as the channel deepens and the banks grow
steeper and closer together.
name: Swiftly Flowing Anduin

desc:
The current here is swift and unrelenting, flowing in from the east and away to
the south. Tall, golden-topped trees loom high upon the sloped riverbanks that
rise to the north and west of the wide river. Far to the east, the woodlands are
of a different breed than the majestic mellyrn of Lorien. The eastern forests
are tall and healthy in their own right, but somehow lack the timeless and
magical air of that elven forest of legend.
name: Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The current here is swift and powerful, quickly carrying floating objects away
through a wide bend in the river. In contrast to the great mallorn-forest that
rises high above the western bank, the woodlands bordering the eastern shore
consist of smaller, younger beeches, larches, poplars and birches. Immediately
above the eastern riverbank, a small clearing with a carpet of soft grass and
wildflowers interrupts the forest continuum.
name: A Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The waters of the Anduin enter a smooth bend, flowing swiftly from the east and
away to the south. On either side of the river the lush, green banks rise
steadily, giving way to tall woodlands. Far to the west, the trees grow even
higher and seem to be crowned in gold.
name: Swiftly Flowing River

desc:
The stream here is shallow and noisy as it falls down through rocky crevices and
silty pools. Not far to the east the small waterway merges with the wide,
powerful waters of the river Anduin, which flows steadily southward between
forested banks. Tall mallorn-trees rise above the high northern bank. Westward,
a small trail runs parallel to the stream.
name: Tumbling Stream

desc:
Grasses and flowers lap at the powerful trunks of the mellyrn that dominate the
landscape. The smooth, golden curves of ancient boughs intermingle above you, so
that not a single inch of sky can be seen above the living ceiling. To the
south, a small stream tumbles swiftly over smooth rocks, its foamy fingers
tickling fertile brown banks. In the west a simple arc of grey stone traverses
the woodland brook. The steep grade of the riverbank, combined with the swift
current would make ascending from the stream to the forest impossible.
name: Mallorn Forest

desc:
In all directions, your view is dominated by the tall, straight trunks of
golden-boughed mellyrn. Every inch of these lush woodlands seem to be filled
with a healthy, thriving species of wildlife. Despite this abundance of
organisms, the forest does not feel constricted or over-populated. Rather, every
living thing, from tiny blade of grass to impossibly ancient tree, exists in
harmony.
name: Thriving Mallorn Forest

desc:
This forest is a model of dichotomy. Tall, thick mallorn-trees, rooted in this
earth for thousands of years, live side by side with the tiniest flower whose
bloom lasts for no more than a season. The downward slope of the land becomes
more pronounced to the east and south, where the sound of running water is the
loudest.
name: Rising Forest

desc:
The wide trunks of golden-crested mellyrn rise above you in all directions.
Flourishing under watchful shadows of these ancient trees are countless tiny
plants and flowers. Every colour of the rainbow seems to be represented in a
dozen shades, although the dominant hues are of green, grey, and gold. Looking
up, the trunks disappear into a sky of intertwining limbs and broad leaves. A
mighty river can barely be seen through the undergrowth to the east, sliding
powerfully between the sloping banks.
name: Tall Woodland

desc:
Massive mallorn-trees loom proudly in all directions, like thick-walled towers
topped with gold-encrusted battlements. This forest doesn't just look ancient,
but rather seems to radiate oldness. The strong and graceful trees thrive upon a
power that quite obviously encompasses this entire land. Slender shoots and
delicate flowers provide poignant contrast to these silent giants. Every now and
then the faint sound of music reaches your ears, but you can not tell if it is
voice or instrument that yields these ageless melodies.
name: Mallorn Forest

desc:
The long, thin limbs of beech, larch and poplar arch gracefully outwards above
the clear waters of the river Anduin. The forest floor is covered by soft,
organic soil so brown that it is almost black, from which sprout a thousand
varieties of flowering plants that grow right up to the river's edge. The
woodlands continue east and south as far as the eye can see.
name: Forest at the Water's Edge

desc:
These woodlands are young and healthy, comprised mainly of small deciduous trees
and thick, flowering shrubs. Patches of sky are visible high above a ceiling of
long, sloping tree-limbs. Every so often a grassy clearing interrupts the forest
continuum, and here and there a large boulder lies sunken into the soft ground,
layered with years of moss and lichen.
name: Beneath the Boughs

desc:
The land here can't be summarised as either a forest or a field, but rather
marks a transition between the two. Thick, soft grasses, deep enough to cover a
grown man's knees, lap gently at the thin trunks of the prospering trees that
sprout happily from the rich soil. To the west the forest thickens; a faint
sound of rushing water can be heard from that direction. In the east the trees
become more sparse, giving way to vast plains.
name: Mingling of Forest and Grassland

desc:
A cluster of birch trees, their bark a clean shade of white, sit upon a small
incline bordered to the north and west by the stately waters of the river
Anduin. The forest floor is soft and healthy, woven from a dozen species of
soft, broad-bladed grass. To the east, several other varieties of small to
medium-sized deciduous trees mingle with the thin, peeling birch. Continuing
south, the ground slopes steeply upward, and the sound of the river is louder
and more violent.
name: Riverside Woodland

desc:
The hardwood trees growing here are thin-trunked and well-spaced, so that large
patches of sky are visible beyond their rounded tops. The forest floor is a
brilliant shade of green, covered completely by a short, broad-bladed species of
grass. Flowers of multiple pastel colours dot the spongy turf, lifted above
ground level by slender shoots of green and brown.
name: Thinly Wooded Meadow

desc:
No tree sprouts from these grasslands, but the effect is certainly not one of a
wide, never-ending plain. To the north, west, and south, trees and grasses may
be seen mingling, giving way eventually to healthy hardwood forests. Only to the
east do the plains extend uninterrupted by such taller forms of plant life.
name: Edge of the Plains

desc:
The grassy vegetation becomes taller and more verdant as the broad plains drift
closer to the fertile soil of the woodlands which lie some ways to the north and
west. Conversely, moving eastwards the height of the grass decreases and the
dominant colour of the landscape shifts from green to yellow. Far to the west,
the level of the horizon rises slightly, indicating that a thicker forest grows
in that direction.
name: Plains Nearing the Woodlands

desc:
The land rolls gently away in all four directions, covered by thick grasses of
several varieties. The hue of the vegetation is much greener to the north and
west, however. Here each blade of grass is a sun-bleached shade of yellow,
wilting towards the ground so that the overall height of the plant-life is quite
low.
name: Yellow Grasses

desc:
A sparse but healthy forest lies to the north and west, framing these treeless
lands into a corner of the wide plain which extends to the southeast. Several
varieties of grass sprout from the fertile, hilly earth. Most obvious of these
is a tall, wide-bladed species, which sprouts in thick bunches and reaches to
heights of up to three feet. Partially hidden by this thick vegetation is
another notable variety of grass, which is soft and spongy in texture and coats
the soil like a sun-loving breed of moss.
name: Northwest Corner of the Plains

desc:
This forest of white-barked birch and grey-trunked larch has thinned as it
meandered eastwards, and here it finally ends. The woodlands which for many
miles have mingled with rolling meadows have been replaced by the grasslands. To
the north, a few trees are still visible within the sea of tall grasses, but to
the south and east the fields continue uninterrupted.
name: Edge of the Woodlands

desc:
A roughly circular grove of young larch and birch dominate this woodland
setting. As they reach to the sky, their thin, flexible limbs intermingle to
form a web-like ceiling of leaves and branches. Some small flowering plants dot
the landscape, but for the most part the forest is carpeted with a layer of
coarse soil.
name: Young Larch and Birch

desc:
The border between forest and plains is surprisingly pronounced. To the north
and west, thin-trunked larch and birch trees sprout from a soft, grassy carpet,
their healthy limbs stretching unopposed towards the sky. At a certain point,
however, this thin forest simply stops, as if an invisible border were drawn on
the ground. Beyond this roughly straight line plains of tall grass coat the
hills and meander eastwards as far as the eye can see.
name: Forest Border

desc:
A handful of lively, young hardwoods sprout from a rolling lawn of thick,
broad-bladed grass. To the south the trees become even more sparse, marking the
beginning of a great plain. Westwards lies a flowery clearing, bordered by thin
woodlands similar to these.
name: Sparse Green Forest

desc:
These woodlands are airy and spacious, spreading out in every direction as far
as the eye can see. The ground is firm and dark brown in colour, spotted green
by the occasional thin-leafed shoot. The deep, resonant sound of a mighty river
drifts in from the northwest, and in this direction the land has a slight
downward slope.
name: Slim Woodland

desc:
The soft earthen floor is painted green by the tall grass that grows abundantly
in this clearing. The ground slopes gently down to the west, where the Anduin
flows wide and deep. To the north, west, and south, young trees sprout from
similar grasslands.
name: Forest Clearing

desc:
The ancient mallorn trees here stand apart, so that the sky is visible far above
their golden crowns. These trees rise from a gently swaying sea of soft grass
and star-shaped flowers that seem to glimmer and change colour before your eyes.
The sounds of swiftly flowing water and soft, musical voices float through the
air. A small trail opens up in the east, quickly disappearing into the thick
maze of silvery trunks.
name: Light Mallorn Forest

desc:
The golden mellyrn lend a golden hue to the landscape, which seems to glitter
like an ancient treasure hoard. Tall, slender shoots spring from the ground,
culminating in pale green and white bell-shaped flowers. Just to the east, a
faint trail winds gracefully amidst the verdant undergrowth, but there is an
elusive quality about this land that makes it impossible to gain any definitive
sense of its direction.
name: Lush Golden Woodland

desc:
Overhead, mallorn boughs weave amongst each other like slender grey and gold
fingers engaged in a gentle grasp. Beneath this natural ceiling a small trail
weaves through the forest floor, its path having been chosen to avoid the
delicate flowers that sprout from the lush green undergrowth. A rocky brook
tumbles alongside the trail, filling the forest with the sound of water lightly
dancing upon stone. The stream flows away to the east, where a simple stone
bridge rises above the soft, living carpet. The trees are dense in the northern
section of this woodland, but to the south the sky opens up above the path.
name: Streambank Trail

desc:
The sky opens above this circular glade, a small island in the sea of ancient
trees visible in all directions. The ground rises to the south, where widely
spread boughs of the golden-crowned mellyrn seem to reach up like immortal hands
and hold the stars in place. To the north and east an unassuming path dances
amid the slender shoots and jewel-like flowers.
name: Mallorn Forest Glade

desc:
This verdant forest is composed of thick green undergrowth sheltered by tall,
proud mallorn-trees. A narrow trail forms a winding line of dark, organic brown
colour that meanders on a general north-south axis. To the north and east the
gurgles and splashes of a small stream reach your ears. Although the source of
these sounds is hidden behind the wide trunks of the golden-crested mellyrn,
their intensity suggests the running water is quite close.
name: Trail above the Brook

desc:
This bridge is but a narrow arc of weather-beaten stones spanning a lively
brook, which splashes and gurgles over speckled grey stones. Barely two feet
wide, this structure has no posts, railings, or other visible reinforcements.
Nevertheless it is solid and unmoving, a model of simplicity but certainly not
of amateur construction. The stones are ancient and carved to fit so masterfully
that at first glance the bridge appears one giant curve of solid rock. Countless
varieties of moss and lichen cover those areas of the bridge not directly walked
upon, so that the structure almost appears to be just another living organism
sprouting from the soft, fertile soil.
name: On a Small Stone Bridge

desc:
The trail here is narrow and does not intrude far into the surrounding
woodlands. The path is smooth, firm, and slightly concave, making for utmost
ease of travel. From the south the sound of swiftly flowing water reaches your
ears, and a simple stone bridge can be seen rising from the earthen path in that
direction. Stately mallorn-trees rise from the lush green forest floor, their
tops golden and graceful.
name: Path through the Golden Forest

desc:
Running steadily southward towards the Celebrant, the small stream crosses a
forest trail. Small stones have been placed to ease the passage across the swift
waters. The swift stream continues on into the forest, soon disappearing from
view, while the trail marches onwards through the forest towards a distant goal.
name: A Small Ford

desc:
The forest is sparse here, proudly striving towards the sky, as if to catch the
bright stars above. In daytime the light of the heavens dispels all shadows,
nurturing a lush and green undergrowth that has spread out like a soft green
carpet, covering all of the ground that you can see.
name: Egladil

desc:
Sparkling and shining in the light from the stars, the city of Caras Galadhon,
and the sun, a babbling stream emerges from the ground here and starts the long
journey through the forest towards the great rivers. Running amidst the tall
grey trees that are the heart of the Golden Wood, the stream almost seems to
tease the forest it leaves behind.
name: A Sparkling Spring

desc:
The forest grows more sparse here, the mellyrn standing farther apart, allowing
the undergrowth to expand and grow into a lush, flowing carpet that covers and
caresses the soil of Lothlorien. From the south, you can hear the soft babbling
of a small brook and in the distance, you hear faint voices that are carried
through the woods from the green mound of Caras Galadhon.
name: The Forest Border

desc:
The ground here slopes up from the east to the west, where on top of the slope a
road runs through the forest, bending as it approaches the great hill of Caras
Galadhon. A gentle wind blows from the green hilltop, bringing music and sounds
of singing and laughter. A line of white birches rises at the bottom of the
eastward slope, marking the border of the realm of mellyrn and the start of the
ordinary forest. A feeling of remoteness is about the birches, yet they seem to
drink the air of Lorien with every branch and twig.
name: The Forest Border

desc:
Protected by the magic of the Golden Wood, this land has never been touched by
winter frost and knows no sharp transitions between seasons. Whatever the time
of year might be in the world outside, here, on this tree-covered slope, a
feeling of Spring is in the air. The green and the gold of the mallorn trees
contrast with the sombre colours of the dark forest visible at a distance in the
northeast as the young foliage and blossoms of flowering trees in Spring might
contrast with the dark conifers that are still in the grip of Winter. Fresh
scents of grass, earth and water rise from the ground and spread, carried by a
soft wind.
name: The Forested Slope

desc:
The soft forest floor slopes gently downwards to the east, making its way
towards a timeless mallorn tree. The trunk of the great tree is smooth to the
touch and silver-grey, showing no signs of old age. The elegant branches stretch
towards the ground, spreading over a large flat boulder, as if trying to hide it
from strangers. Patches of the sky are rarely visible through the golden-green
foliage, and only a faint glow from above reaches through the tree-tops.
name: The Forested Slope

desc:
Stretching their silvery limbs and shimmering with the golden burden upon them,
the mellyrn stand tall on the forested slope, and yet the sky above their tops
seems to be very close to the ground. Suspended in every opening among the
branches, reflected in every drop of water on a leaf or a blade of grass, the
ever-changing sky seems to be looking straight at the exposed forest floor, as
if the sun, the moon, and the stars of Elbereth were watching the protected
land, guarding it so evil could never enter it under their watchful eye.
name: The Forested Slope

desc:
Half concealed by grass, low shrubs and flowers, a faint trail runs through the
golden forest. To the west it climbs slightly upward, reaching the bank of a
forest road. To the east it slopes down and turns north, continuing on its way
to the outer reaches of Lorien and to the valley of the Great River Anduin.
name: On an Overgrown Trail

desc:
The mellyrn here have grown thick and broad, as if the trees were soldiers
fortifying an invisible rear flank. A soft ray of light slips through the dense,
towering canopy above, before finally coming to rest upon the majestic landscape
below, illuminating the mellyrn with a warm, golden glow. Upon the ground is a
layer of emerald-green moss, seemingly untouched by hoof or foot; it bears no
unnatural scuffs or abrasions, as if a blanket of deep-green colour was draped
over the forest floor to keep the roots warm and cosy.
name: Among a Thick Growth of Mellyrn

desc:
Here, in a small glade by the stream at the northeastern border of Lorien, a
fire is burning on top of a small mound of stones which serves as a simple
hearth. A few dry pine branches and pine cones lie at the base of the mound,
obviously meant to feed the fire, which, however, seems to consume almost no
fuel. Elves fear neither darkness nor shadows, but the Third Age has taught them
the insecurity which used to be the lot of Men. Elves used to have no need of
fire, but in the night, when the outside world approaches the borders of the
Golden Wood and creatures of the dark stand, staring, on the very bank of the
border stream, the fire might serve as a defence, or just as a spot of light and
warmth in the dark.
name: By a Fire

desc:
A swift stream carries its clear waters through a shallow depression, forming a
natural border of the forest of Lorien. The western bank is shrouded with a mist
through which one can only faintly distinguish the shapes of the trees of the
Golden Wood. The stream bends from the north to the east around a large grey
rock which sits deeply in the sandy soil. A few yellow rockroses grow on the
rock and by its sides, their colours strangely bright by contrast to the golden
forest which is almost completely concealed by the mist.
name: The Border Stream

desc:
The tree-trunks in this riverside woodland are thin and well-spaced, so that
visibility is significant in every direction. Looking towards the sky reveals a
season-dependent tapestry of green, red or brown leaves crossed with greyish
white branches, set upon a distant background of blue, grey or black. The swift
waters of the Anduin curve around from north to west, gliding past the forest
which lies just above water level. Scattered among the trees, and along the
river, lie pieces of wood, telling a tale of ancient days.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
The river here is wide and deep, although small white caps visible to the west
indicate that the current picks up speed in that direction. To the east and
south grassy riverbanks give way to a thin forest of birch and poplar that lies
only slightly above water level. Far to the west, across the mighty Anduin, the
ground rises higher and the forest becomes more dense.
name: On the Anduin

desc:
Two arches crossing in the centre support the delicate latticework of this
arbour. Vines with glossy, dark green leaves and large, bell-like flowers cover
it, creating a gentle shade underneath. The flowers are blue and pink on the
sides of the arbour, but those at its ceiling are bright white, reminiscent of
lilies floating on the surface of the cool forest pools. The entrance to the
arbour is to the west, flanked by two slender mallorn trees. Their golden crowns
mingle at the level of the arches and expand westwards, extending the area of
cool green shade.
name: Vine-covered Arbour

desc:
Leading down into the depths of earth is what appears to be at first a ladder,
then a chaotic entanglement of tree roots. The very first steps down the ladder
are smooth, grey roots of two mellyrn that become almost horizontal as they
stretch away from the base of the trees. Descending deeper and to the east,
however, one would have to rely for support on gnarled and twisted roots of
other, seemingly ancient trees. Hollows have formed between and around the roots
here and there, allowing passage into the deeps. A faint, eerie glow shines from
below, suggesting that this descent may lead into a large underground cavern.
name: Among the Roots

desc:
This hollow under the roots of the trees was apparently washed out by an
underground stream that long since dried. Barely lit by phosphorescent fungi
that grow on the floor and on the overhanging roots, it has an eerie, timeless
look. Like a place where magic has warped the world, this cavern seems to lie
entirely outside of time and outside of remembered history, as if the Golden
Wood did not lie just above it and the sun and the stars did not light the sky
above. Yet no magic can be felt in this place that seems to lie like a dark void
below the roots of the enchanted wood.
name: Dark Hollow

desc:
The birches and other small-leaved trees gradually give way to a different kind
of tree here. Mixed with ordinary trees, impressive, grey trees with straight
boles reach towards the sky. Their large limbs stretch out high above, filled
with leaves that seem to shimmer in gold and green hue, whereas their clumped
roots make all paths difficult to travel. Between the large trees, a strange
golden haze lingers, sometimes clearly visible and sometimes barely discernible.
The trees seem to radiate an aura of watchfulness, as if they were guardsmen of
some higher entity.
name: Edge of the Golden Wood

desc:
Along the riverbank, there grow small birches, mixed with larger, ancient trees.
The stream flowing east of here is visible between the boles of the trees; some
of them lean over the water, dipping their branches in the stream. The air is
filled with a sense of peacefulness, and the ground is covered with lush, green
grass that is occasionally interrupted by small patches of white and blue
flowers.
name: A Forested Streambank

desc:
The ground is sloping slightly down towards the small river stream that swiftly
flows from the north to the east of here. The ground is covered with short but
lush grass, occasionally interrupted by small weeds and brightly coloured
flowers. The trees, mostly small birch and ash ones, stand sparsely, allowing
the sky to be visible between the leaves and the slim white-grey limbs.
name: Sloping Streambank

desc:
Here, the stream to the north has carved a large ledge in the cliff, forming a
natural overhang. Erosion by water and wind has cleared off the soil, leaving
the bedrock bare at the edge of the cliff. Apart from some low bushes and a few
patches of weed, this area is open in contrast to the dense forest that grows to
the south and the west of here.
name: An Outcrop Of Bedrock

desc:
Here, the landscape is level and covered with a carpet of low green grass that
thrives in the soft, rich soil. Apart from a sparse population of mixed
soft-leafed trees that are strewn about, the area is open. A trail leads to a
slender stone bridge to the east and stretches south alongside the edge of a
foreboding, massive forest to the west of here. The trail is easy to follow even
though it looks ancient and rarely trodden.
name: Trail between a Bridge and a Forest

desc:
The faint trail makes a turn between north and west here. The lightly forested
plains that stretch out here stand in sharp contrast to the ominous large grey
trees that dominate the area to the west. Birches, ash and elm trees grow
scattered about on the grassy plains. West of here, the trail disappears from
sight as it continues into a forest. The distinctive edge of the forest forms a
formidable border, like the stony vertical surface of a castle wall which looks
as if it could serve well as a defence line against unwanted intruders. The
branches of the trees hang very low, as if waiting to knock careless riders off
their mounts.
name: Trail along the Forest Border

desc:
The grassy trail continues north and south between the impressive mallorn trees.
The trees stand tall and silent with their grey boles and the green and silvery
foliage, but still radiating an aura of watchfulness. No sounds are heard here,
but the sense of a larger presence is abundant in the air, as if the trees were
able to communicate with each other and to relay warnings about unwelcome
intruders to other watchmen.
name: Winding Trail in the Golden Wood

desc:
Here, a faint trail coming from the south meets the almost overgrown trail which
runs in a due east and west direction between the giant mallorn trees. The soft
ground is covered with lush green grass and small many-coloured flowers. Even in
the middle of the trail path, the grass grows surprisingly healthy and high, as
if it had never been trampled by cartwheels or heavy feet. Westwards, the trail
continues towards the interior of Lorien, and to the east, the forest seems to
yield to more open areas.
name: Trail Branch in the Golden Wood

desc:
No hedge or other visible boundary separates the mallorn forest from the
surrounding woods, yet the border of the Golden Wood is quite distinct. As the
forest crosses this invisible line, mellyrn give way to broadleaved trees of a
forest as ordinary and as soothingly familiar as the walls and the roof of one's
dwelling might look when one wakes up from dreaming of a beautiful, yet unknown
land under an unfamiliar sky.
name: Outer Reaches of the Mallorn Forest

desc:
Tall, proud mallorn trees, their crowns ladled with shimmering golden leaves,
grow throughout this area. Overhead, through the thick branches, patches of sky
allow the sun to caress the small, delicate flowers that sprout in the rich
soil. Mixed with the large golden trees, young birches thrive, leaning in
towards their elders as if enchanted by their presence.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
The grassy trail turns between north and east as it meanders between the massive
grey boles of the huge mallorn trees that grow densely together. The crowns of
the majestic trees shimmer in green and gold, manifesting a peaceful haven, to
which no troubles from the outside world can reach. A healthy green grass
blanket, mixed with flowers that sparkle in all colours of the rainbow, covers
the ground.
name: Trail in the Golden Forest

desc:
Between the tall mallorn trees, the small trail continues in a due north and
south direction. Half concealed by grass and low flowers, the trail is almost
hidden at times. Nevertheless, finding the right way is simple, as if the trees
themselves guided the wanderer who strays from the right path. High above,
between the magnificent tree crowns, a yellow smoke-like mist lingers. At times,
faint harmonies are heard, carried by a soft wind from the west.
name: Faint Trail in the Golden Wood

desc:
The giant trees grow densely here, and it is easy to lose one's sense of
direction in this maze-like forest. All paths are very narrow and impossible to
follow on horseback. The trees almost seem to be alive, even though their grey
boles are firmly rooted in the rich, soft soil. High above, their limbs
intertwine, and the leaves form an artificial roof that shimmers in silver, gold
and green. Between the trees a golden mist lingers, sometimes barely
discernible. At times, strange harmonies can be heard faintly, as if they were
carried by a soft wind. The ground is covered with fallen leaves that glitter as
if they were made of pure gold.
name: Deep Mallorn Forest

desc:
This appears to be the eastern reach of the Golden Wood. The mallorn forest
extends to the west, but eastward a more common forest in shades of green, brown
and grey continues on its way down the slope. Everywhere the vegetation is quite
lush and tangled, making the terrain difficult to travel. A sense of uncertainty
and incompleteness pervades the scene, as if neither of the two forests could
entirely take over this land nor give it up, for it is here that the winds of
Lorien and of the outer lands meet and mingle. The wind of Lorien is warm and
bears scents of flowers and orchards whereas the wind from the east brings the
clear and cold air of river valleys and pine forests.
name: Border of the Mallorn Forest

desc:
Here, the east and the west trails through the forest are augmented by another
smaller trail which branches off to the south into what appears to be a small
glade in the otherwise dense mallorn forest. The large grey trees stand straight
and tall here like silent watchmen on duty. Their large crowns spread out high
above, creating an artificial roof of leaves that shimmer in gold and green,
whereas the lower branches hang just at the right height to stop anyone from
riding through this intersection. Between the grey limbs, a yellow and golden
mist lingers, sometimes barely discernible.
name: Branch of Trails

desc:
This secluded forest glade is surrounded by large, golden-crested mallorn trees.
Trails extend in several directions, suggesting that the area is a nexus for
those who travel this forest. Even though there are no structures or other
obvious signs of activity here, a sense of presence can be felt. Every now and
then, faint melodies float through the air, lingering for a moment until they
blend with the sounds of nature.
name: Small Glade in the Mallorn Forest

desc:
The massive grey mallorn trees grow more sparsely here, allowing room for a mix
of birches and large ash trees to thrive in the open spaces. In a cascade of
green and golden colours, the mallorn trees reflect the light cast upon them.
The rich, fertile ground is home to lush green hay which is tall enough to make
passage difficult, and countless small flowers that seem to radiate in every
colour of the rainbow.
name: Radiating Mallorn Forest

desc:
A faint trail runs east and west between the high trees with golden and silvery
leaves. The trail is sometimes concealed by grass and flowers, but still easy to
follow, perhaps by instinct. Obviously, this trail is very seldom trodden by
anyone else than the creatures that inhabit this land. The large trees grow
closely together here, and like silent watchmen they stand, emitting an aura of
vigilance and alertness.
name: Trail in the Golden Forest

desc:
At first gently, then more steeply until it levels out again, the forest floor
slopes downward to the east. The golden forest follows the slope until its
lowest point, where the mellyrn come to a halt at a clump of tall firs. Like
guardians at the boundary of another world, the fir trees stand dark and solemn,
a faint silvery shimmer around their dark branches.
name: Border of the Golden Forest

desc:
Several different species of trees have found their place here as tall grey
mallorn trees gradually give way to a more common forest consisting of birch,
elm and other smaller trees. At some places, the trees stand further apart,
creating small glades that allow sunlight to reach the ground. To the east and
the north along the bank of the stream that is seen between the tree trunks,
bushes and shrubs thrive.
name: A Light Mixed Forest

desc:
A mixture of scattered firs, small pinetrees and patches of weed defines this
somewhat boggy area, which is limited to the north and east by the swift stream
that cuts through the landscape. The ground here is low, at some places almost
the same level as the stream, and it might easily be flooded if the tide is
high.
name: A Lightly Forested Streambank

desc:
A small but well-defined trail runs due east and west between the trees. The
healthy grass that covers the ground shimmers in all shades of green. Smaller
birches are mixed with larger trees with a grey, smooth bark and leaves that
seem to be made of gold. The large trees, which become more common farther west
of here, look foreboding as if they were ready to crush any undesired visitor to
these lands.
name: Trail through a Light Forest

desc:
This massive bridge made of grey stone blocks gracefully arches over the swift
river in order to provide a safe crossing for travellers in these lands. Unlike
the bridges built elsewhere, this stony course seems to have sprung from the
earth itself, showing perfect harmony with surrounding nature. The stones are
skilfully crafted and fit together to achieve a perfectly flat surface, as the
walkway is without any parapets but wide enough for two carts to meet. Moss and
lichen growth on the foundations reveal that the bridge has withstood many years
without the slightest sign of decay.
name: A Slender Bridge over the Stream

desc:
The trail travels along the riverside where the landscape allows easy passage to
the grass-covered bank. A small river flowing from the north-west joins the path
of the mighty Anduin just to the south of here, forming unpredictable currents
in the wide river. The trail comes to a stop on the eastern riverbank of the
Anduin, where a small pebbled beach stretches out along the shallow waterline.
name: Small Trail along the Riverbanks

desc:
The plains are mostly level; only to the south-east, there is a small downward
slope towards a swiftly flowing river that passes by. The ground is mostly
covered with green patches of grass that grow almost waist-high. Evidently, the
the close proximity of water around creates a hospitable environment for the
vegetation in this area.
name: Grassy Flats beside the River

desc:
A mixture of tall, proud mallorn trees and smaller deciduous trees such as ash
and birch dominate the vegetation here. While not as magnificent as the ancient
mellyrn, they seem enchanted by their company and look taller and healthier here
than in most other areas. To the east of here, the shoreline of a river forms a
natural boundary to the golden forest.
name: Outer Reaches of the Golden Wood

desc:
The soft ground is almost boggy at some places due to the ample supply of water.
The green, healthily growing grass is high, mixed with various reeds that thrive
wherever small puddles have formed in the watery ground. The steep banks of the
Great River Anduin are rocky and lined with low brushwood and occasional lesser
trees.
name: Plains by the Riverbanks

desc:
The tall patches of grass that grow on these fields resemble a thick green
blanket which stretches out in all directions. The most common types of
vegetation, apart from the dominating grass, seem to be low shrubs and other
hardy species. In the distance to the south, the shimmering surface of a large
river cuts through the grassland.
name: Grass-covered Riverside Plains

desc:
The ground here is green and lush due to the ample supply of water. Long-leaved
grass grows all over the fields, mixed with small evergreen trees. The landscape
slopes down slightly towards the riverbanks of a wide river to the south and the
east of here. Behind the bush-lined riverbanks, the river passes by, carrying an
endless flow of water southwards.
name: Fields by the Great River

desc:
The ground here is firm, and it seems to be composed mostly of sand and dry
soil. The pale green patches of grass seem to absorb whatever moisture their
roots might be able to find. Occasionally, thick shrubs decorate the
grass-covered fields that open wide in all directions. To the south of here, the
plains slope down towards a large river which can be seen in the distance.
name: Sandy Ground

desc:
The landscape here is lush and green, clearly thriving in the ample supply of
water from the neighbouring river. Apart from a few small, bushy depressions,
the terrain is level with a slight descent down towards the riverbanks. Small
stands of man-high birches grow scattered in the area, providing both food and
protection for the grazing animals that roam this area.
name: Fields by the Riverbanks

desc:
The ample supply of water from the large river that flows by to the east and to
the south of here clearly has an influence on the landscape and the vegetation
in this area. At places, the ground is boggy and damp. High stands of reeds grow
on the wet places. At the more dry areas lush grey-green grass covers the
ground. To the south and the west of here, the plains dip slightly down to the
riverbanks, where stands of bushes line the shore.
name: Riverside Plains

desc:
Small birches grow in scattered clumps here, occasionally interrupting the
grey-green field of grass. The climate is obviously mild enough and the soil
fertile enough to allow also other plants than grass to root here. A few small
bushes have sprung up as well, providing protection for smaller animals. To the
north-west of here, a small trail can be seen as it curves around a low hill
that forms a distinguishable landmark in these plains.
name: Field of Birches

desc:
The small trail makes a gentle turn between north and west as it curves around
the hills that rise above the surrounding landscape west of here. The green
grass grows a few feet high and looks healthy enough to provide nourishment for
a variety of grazing animals. The fields to the south-east of the trail descend
in a slight slope down towards the bare riverbanks of a wide river far in the
distance.
name: Trail Bend

desc:
As the trail continues in a due east and west direction, it passes a small hill
that lies like a small ship in a sea of grass. Some vague tracks in the dirt
seem to be of the animals living in the grassland which prefer to use the trail
instead of trudging through the grass. The tracks and various droppings indicate
that these fields are home to several kinds of grazing animals as well as a few
predators. Even though the trail seems to be seldom used today, small stones
erected along the trailside make it easy to follow it across the open land.
name: Viewpoint in the Trail

desc:
The faint trail carries on in a due east and west direction, crossing the
grass-covered plains that dominate the view in all directions from here. The
trail, seldom used, is no more that a small depression in the grass. In past
times, however, this was probably a major route which was travelled frequently.
Small grey milestones are erected at the side of the trail.
name: Trail in the Field

desc:
The water that spills in from the stream to the west and the south of here is
almost flooding these low plains. The water-drenched ground is soft and gives
away easily, and the footing is almost treacherous at some places. Here and
there, small puddles have formed between stands of reed and sturdy weeds. To the
east of here, the ground is slightly more elevated and more firm.
name: Swampy Grassland

desc:
Thick grass covers these plains like a green blanket. Some scattered patches of
high reaching flowers and a few thorny bushes are the only things that break the
monotone grey-green impression. To the north, a steep, bush-growing slope climbs
up the side of a low hill, and the ridge blocks the further view to that way. In
all other directions, the flat green fields continue to the horizon.
name: Green Fields by the Trail

desc:
These lands mainly consist of open plains, mixed with small scattered brushwood.
The black soil seems to provide the support for a variety of different green
plants, from long blades of grass to small decidous trees. The ground has a
slight tilt down towards the bank of the stream that flows east of here, its
water washing the riverbank which is lined with rocks and gravel.
name: Fields beside the River

desc:
This is obviously a crossing, with trails leading away in several directions.
The ground is well-trampled and firm from years of use, although the grass has
now reclaimed the areas that were once bare soil. To the north, a small
dwindling path leads off into the vast grassland. In the eastward direction, a
larger trail stretches out, although overgrown in places and sparsely used. To
the west, a small bridge spans the stream that swiftly flows southwards. Just
beside the head of the bridge, a large flat stone has been erected.
name: Trail Crossing at a Bridge

desc:
This slender bridge was obviously built by skilled craftsmen, since it has
withstood the forces of water and weather for many years. It gracefully arches
over the swift stream below to provide a safe passage for travellers. The
walkway is constructed with sturdy planks of wood onto the rigid system of the
supporting columns. Well-crafted handrails provide good support when walking
across the bridge.
name: A Slender Bridge

desc:
The small trail continues in a slight slope down towards the bank of the swift
stream that flows just to the west of here. The sound of the rushing water is
constantly present as a backdrop in the otherwise quiet landscape. The carpet of
lush green grass is only interrupted by a few scattered bushes and small
deciduous trees that thrive in the dark soil nourished by the ample supply of
water.
name: Trail by the Stream

desc:
The small, dirty trail is nothing more but a strip of bare soil with some
scattered pebbles and small rocks, stretching across the seemingly endless
yellowish-green fields. The trail continues in a due north-south direction
without significant deviation. Across the plains to the east of here, nothing
but some scattered bushes and undulating hills break the monotone landscape. A
short distance away to the west of here, a swift stream rushes by southwards.
name: Trail along a Small Stream

desc:
The trail continues to the north and the south and is almost overgrown by grass,
making it difficult to find the right path between the bushes and the high green
weeds. The slight depression in the ground caused by previous travellers is
sometimes the most visible evidence of what once might have been a
well-travelled path. To the west, in the distance behind a small stream of
water, the silhouette of a massive forest can barely be seen.
name: Faint, Winding Trail

desc:
The swift stream that rushes by just to the west of here provides plenty of
water to allow the green grass that thrives here to grow almost waist-high. The
healthy vegetation here should provide ample support for grazing animals. To the
south, the constant erosion by the fast-flowing water has created a steep gully
with abrupt edges.
name: Plains by the Stream

desc:
The plains are covered with thick, long grass that reaches almost waist-high.
The ground here is dry and firm, offering little support for much else than the
dominating grass and a few scattered shrubberies. Although there is no sign of
animals or other living creatures here, some small holes in the ground and a few
scattered tracks suggest that this area is not entirely deserted.
name: Empty Plains

desc:
The small, earthen path stretches out in a due north-south direction. At times,
it is merely a faint trail between the yellow-green, long grass. The earthy
surface has formed a slight depression that follows the east-west route in the
middle of the slightly undulating landscape. To the north, the trail continues
between bushes and undulating low hills until it is lost in the high grass that
dominates the landscape. In all the other directions, there only grow some dry
grass and occasionally, a few scattered patches of heather.
name: Trail in the Field

desc:
The yellowish grass that covers most of the ground here stands a few feet high.
Apart from a few scattered juniper bushes and patches of weed, nothing else but
grass has managed to root in the poor soil of this area. On the surface, some
trails of a long dried stream are still visible, as it has eroded the ground on
its turning point from its eastern course to the south. To the west of here, a
small meandering trail stretches out over the undulating fields. To the north
and the east, nothing but the yellow-green sea of grass can be seen as it
extends to the horizon.
name: Fields beside the Trail

desc:
The plains stretch out here, forming a yellowish sea of grass that seems to
heave with every gust of wind. Signs of wildlife are sparse here, only a few
holes and some faint tracks in the dry ground suggest that this area is not
entirely devoid of life. Further to the north, the grassy fields turn into
higher, gently undulating hills.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
The yellowish dry grass that grows almost waist-high, dominates the open field
that stretches out in all directions. The dry soil seems to be unable to offer
support for anything else than the most robust plants. Nevertheless, a few
withered juniper bushes have managed to root here. To the north, the sea of
grass continues towards the horizon.
name: Yellow, Dry Plains

desc:
The ground here is even drier than in the surrounding areas. The soil is bare,
only interrupted by some patches of weed and some of dry, yellow and brown
grass. Some remains of huge roots can still be seen half buried in the dusty
surface, revealing the evidence of the past lushness of these fields. Some
leafless skeletons of small, thorny bushes can be seen growing from the weak
shadow created by any larger rock lying on the ground.
name: Dusty Plains

desc:
The terrain here is flat and without any discernible features. The yellowish,
waist-high grass covers the ground, only interrupted by some patches of weeds
and a few occasional juniper bushes. The vegetation reveals that the rainfall in
this area is sparse, although some tracks on the ground reveal that the area is
not entirely deserted. To the north, the yellow-green sea of grass stretches out
towards the horizon. In all other directions, the landscape continues as lightly
undulating hills.
name: Dry Fields

desc:
The weak trail continues meandering in its east and west course. At times, it is
merely a small depression in the green grass as nature gradually reclaims the
area. At a few places, the size of the trail indicates that many years ago, this
was most likely a well-travelled path, but now, the only recent signs of life
seem to stem from the wild animals that roam these grasslands.
name: Trail in the Field

desc:
The yellow and green grass grows almost waist-high in this area. The tall blades
create a sea that shifts in wave-like patterns with every gust of wind. Like
small islands, lone patches of brush grow in several places. The endless grass
continues to the north and the east, while the ground to the west slightly
descends into a low valley. The ground is somewhat lower to the south where the
fields are interrupted by a small trail that stretches out in an east-west
direction.
name: Sea of Grass

desc:
The terrain here is mostly flat and only broken by a few scattered undulating
hills with gentle slopes. The area is covered with lush grass that grows waist-
high in some places. Scattered animal droppings on the ground also give evidence
that smaller grass eating animals inhabit this area. Occasional rustling in the
grass reveals that small animals thrive in the protection given by the
vegetation.
name: Green Plains

desc:
With no nearby rivers to feed the vegetation, the grass tends to look drier here
than to the east. No flowers or other green plants have rooted in this area.
Only occasional patches of weed and small thorny bushes interrupt the dominating
grassland. Looking in the northern and western directions from here, the plains
looks even more dry and desolate.
name: Dry Grassland

desc:
The ground is sunken here, forming a canyon-like formation in the middle of the
sandy fields. In the bottom of the depression, the soil lies bare without any
larger vegetation, only some small tussocks of dry grass decorate the surface.
Cracks scar the dry soil at the lowest point of the canyon, leaving some small
rocks half buried in the surface. The stones are round in shape, telling their
story about rushing waters modifying the landscape once in the past.
name: Turning Valley in the Plains

desc:
The poor, yellow grass strives to survive in the dusty soil that covers the
ground. Patches of small, dry bushes and weeds are scattered among the grass. A
farmer would have a hard time growing anything here if he were foolish enough to
try to cultivate these desolate plains. Only those animals well-adapted to this
environment have the slightest chance of survival here.
name: Barren Fields

desc:
Dry yellowish grass grows here, mixed with patches of sturdy weeds and other
hardened species. The ground seems somewhat more fertile here compared to the
dry fields to the east, but the soil is still quite dry. The landscape reveals
that some water has been flowing here in the past, turning its course from the
north to the east at this place. To the west, the landscape is greener and more
fertile.
name: Yellow Fields

desc:
The dry grass grows knee-high here, covering the dusty ground with a pale
yellow-green carpet. While the earth is dry, it seems to be more fertile in this
area compared to the dry and barren impressions that are given by the vast
plains to the east of here. West of here, a small track winds its way to the
north and south.
name: Grass-covered Fields

desc:
Here, the pale green grass grows almost waist-high at some places. The soil is
obviously more fertile in this area than in the distant brown plains that
stretch out to the north-east of here. Just south of here, a visible trail
crosses the fields in a due east and west direction, while to the west, a small
narrow dirt track twists back and forth along the north and the south. The two
trails seem to conjugate in a large, plain area to the south-west.
name: Green Fields beside the Trail

desc:
This small ridge extrudes from the hill to the east of here. The ground here
slightly rises to the east where the top of the hill can be seen. Short yellow
patches of grass that seem to be constantly beaten by weather conditions grow on
the hillsides. To the west and north, the ridge levels out and disappears in the
undulating grasslands.
name: A Small Ridge

desc:
The plains in this area are covered with light green and yellow grass that grows
a few feet high. As the distance to nearby rivers and other sources of water
increases, the ground turns less fertile. This is more evident in the bleak
plains to the north and the east of here, where the meagre vegetation shifts to
yellow-brown colours.
name: Flat and Dry Fields

desc:
With the past years, rushing waters have eaten their way deep into the hillside
to the south, forcing the flow to find an alternative way past the higher
ground. The soft, sandy ground has given away under the pressure of the flow,
slowly eroding into a steeply sloped valley. Now, the bottom of the valley is
dry, giving life only to some small bushes and various small patches of grass.
name: Sandy Valley

desc:
The valley continues to the west, while the surrounding slopes get lower and
lower towards the east. The grass grows quite strong here, indicating the change
in the fertility of the soil. The steep slopes are covered with small bushes and
various kinds of taller grass which can root in the sandy ground. A small trail
follows the path of the valley near to the south, occasionally disappearing in
the thick grass.
name: Valley by the Trail

desc:
The gentle slopes meet here, forming a natural top for this highest hillock in
the nearby grassland. Dry, yellow grass grows only a few of inches high here,
maybe as a result of the dominating weather conditions. The elevated position
here provides a good view over the nearby surroundings. The pale gleam of the
Great River Anduin can be seen in the far distance to the east and south,
cutting its way through the grassy plains. Down on the southern slope, a weak
path can be noticed meandering along the hillside. On the summit of the hill, a
small stone building has been erected.
name: Top of a Small Hill

desc:
The small trail makes a turn to the south and the east here, finding a way
around the hill that rises just to the west of here. The trail was once well-
trampled, even though most of the tracks look many years old. Like an output of
some sort, a small stone building crowns the top of the hill. A small path
branches off the trail here and winds westwards along the hillside.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Thick and lush, green grass covers this area and provides food and shelter for
the numerous small animals that live here. Small holes are dug in the ground at
several places, and the soft soil reveals several tracks of different size. The
ground steeply rises to the north, forming a natural ridge, rising against the
side of a low hill. To the west, the ground becomes even more lush, as the
humidity in the soil increases.
name: Green Fields

desc:
The trail, which heads in a straight east and west direction, is sometimes
difficult to follow in the high, grey-green grass. At times, it becomes just a
small depression in the green field. Evidently, this path is seldom used these
days, although it may once have been a well-trodden track. At some places along
the trail, where the wild grass has not yet reclaimed the land, old tracks of
both horse shoes and cartwheels are still visible in the bare, packed soil.
name: Faint Trail in the Grass

desc:
The stream becomes more violent and roiled as the water is forced to pass a
narrow gully here between the rocky walls to the south-west and the steep earthy
slopes on the northern side. The once crystal-clear water is gradually taking on
a brown hue as it picks up dirt and loose soil from the steep hillsides. To the
south, the flow becomes less turbulent again as the stream encounters more level
land.
name: Stream in a Narrow Gully

desc:
Here, a solid rock wall to the south forces the stream to change its direction.
As the water crashes against the wall, foam-topped waves and unpredictable
swirls form in the current. The relentless erosion by water has formed a gully
between the solid rocks on the southern side and the soft, earthy ground east of
here. Loose rocks and stones that have fallen from the rock wall litter the
streambed and block the flow of water.
name: Roaring Bend in the Stream

desc:
The stream widens out as it exits the narrow gully to the north. The flow is
less turbulent now, but still, the water is agitated by the numerous rocks and
old branches that the stream has brought with it and that now litter the banks
of the stream. The water is humus-coloured and dark, making the it difficult to
see through it.
name: A Swift Stream

desc:
The stream passes under a portal of wood here, which forms the lower side of a
bridge. All sounds are amplified and echoed by the artificial roof, creating a
hollow atmosphere. Supporting stone columns stand on the banks on both sides of
the stream. Lichen and moss have attached to the flat grey surfaces, and brown
horizontal lines on the columns indicate varying water levels.
name: Under a Bridge

desc:
The stream flows more calmly, but still swiftly, as it encounters the flat
terrain that dominates the landscape in this area. The stream makes a wide turn
to the east around the light forest to the south of here. The water of the
stream, which originates from sources high up in the Misty Mountains, was once
crystal-clear but has now acquired a humus-coloured hue.
name: Bend in the Border Stream

desc:
The stream turns to the east here as it makes its way between the light forest
on the western bank of the stream and the open fields on the eastern side. The
stream is wide and flows quickly, although without visible swirls or currents.
Occasionally, small logs and tree limbs float by in the dark water. The banks of
the stream are lined by small groves of reed and patches of long-stemmed weeds.
name: Bend in the Border Stream

desc:
The stream makes a turn to the south to resume its main direction, after a small
detour around the lightly forested areas to the south-west of here. As the once
narrow mountain stream is fed by numerous small creeks from the plains, it
gradually grows and forms a wide and deep river. The water is humus-coloured,
and the bottom of the stream is hidden from sight.
name: Bend in the Border Stream

desc:
The border stream marks the boundary between the open lands to the east and the
large forest that grows to the west. The stream which once originated as a small
creek high up in the Misty Mountains is now wide like a river, as it has been
fed by many small creeks on its way to the ocean far away. The low banks are
lined with patches of reed and grass.
name: The Border Stream

desc:
The stream makes a turn to the south again as it meanders through the landscape.
The plains to the north and the east of here are low and would be flooded if the
water level rose significantly. The banks to the east are lined with pebbles and
small rocks as the stream current has washed away any loose material. To the
south of here, the stream widens and flows more calmly.
name: At the Bend in the Border Stream

desc:
The Great River Anduin flows steadily to the south here. While the river itself
flows rather calmly, the small river just to the west of here feeds a large
amount of water into the Anduin, causing turbulent swirls and unpredictable
currents that might be treacherous even to the most stable vessel. The water is
dark and murky, and the bottom of the river cannot be seen.
name: A Confluence in the Anduin

desc:
The Great River flows more calmly but still steadily as it bends from its
straight western course to the south. While no visible swirls or any turbulent
water can be seen, the sheer power of the flowing mass of water is felt. Along
the shallow riverbanks on the western side a small pebble beach stretches out,
looking like a suitable landing for boats.
name: Wide Section of the Anduin

desc:
The river deviates from its present western course in order to take on a
dominating direction to the south from here. On the stony riverbanks only some
dark, stunted bushes and weak trees grow, providing a sharp contrast to the
otherwise lush, green landscape that surrounds the river in this area. To the
south of here, the river widens up considerably as it takes on a more steady
course to the west again. Upstream to the east of here, the agitated water is
thrown between the rocks and stones as the river winds.
name: Bend in the Anduin

desc:
Here, the river widens up as it takes on a more southern course again. The water
is still stirred by the many rocks that protrude from the riverbed and branches
and roots of small bushes that dip in the water along the shoreline. To the
south of here, the river seems to flow more calm than in the violent bends to
the east.
name: Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The sparse forest that grows in this area contains a mixture of taller deciduous
trees and smaller brush-like trees which thrive in the light that penetrates the
high ceiling of long tree-limbs. In the rich soil, a wide variety of flowering
shrubs grows mixed with small bushes, many of which probably carry edible
berries in the right season. The forested riverbanks approach the wide river to
the north and the west.
name: Forested Riverbank

desc:
This light sparse forest, mainly defined by various smaller deciduous trees
mixed with thick lush grass that grows in the open spaces between the trunks,
stretches out over the flat land. The soil here is rich and provides ample
nourishment for many kinds of flowers and green plants that thrive among the
woods. The course of a wide river can be seen close to the north, and grassy
plains open to the east, while the forest continues to the west and the south.
name: Border of the Forest

desc:
The trail, coming from the east, ends here at the riverbank of the Great River
Anduin. The river is wide here, making the current less turbulent. The
riverbanks are low and occasionally on the very same level as the river itself.
A small natural pool has been formed in the shoal water close to the riverbanks,
lined with a beach of pebbles and gravel. This place could serve well as a
natural landing for travellers using boats in the Anduin.
name: Natural Landing at the Riverbank

desc:
The river twists and bends from the north to the west as it cuts its way through
the green plains. The riverbanks to the east and the south are less steep than
the ones on the inner edge of the bend. The banks are covered with rough gravel
and small, round blocks, and some small pine trees decorate the slopes. Beyond
the surface of the dark, calm but turbulent waters, nothing can be seen expect
for a few larger stone blocks that reach up from the riverbed below.
name: Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The Great River is at its widest here, thus the water flow is relatively calm.
One might be deceived by the seemingly domestic river; however under the calm
surface, the dark water possesses forces that are to be reckoned. Even the most
stable vessels might easily be swept away by the unpredictable currents if in
the hands of unskilled boatsmen.
name: Calm Section of the Anduin

desc:
The Great River continues its majestic flow as it turns its course from the
south to the west. While the river is wide and calm here, the riverbed narrows,
and the water picks up speed again as the river enters the woodlands to the
west. On the eastern shore, a light mixed forest dominates the sloping ground.
name: Wide Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The stream has finally found its way here from the sources high up the Misty
Mountains. It ends in a small delta, emptying its water into the Great River
Anduin. The turbulent flow creates swirls and powerful, treacherous currents as
the water of the two rivers mingles. The swift flow of the Anduin to the east
and the south of here grabs everything that floats out from the river and
quickly carries it downstream.
name: Mouth of the Border Stream

desc:
The swift stream makes its way below a beautifully arched stone bridge here.
Evidence of changes in water level can be seen on the surface of the grey stones
supporting the bridge, showing clearly that in the past, the river has been
carrying more water than it does today. Light green mosses thrive on the flat
surfaces above the waterline.
name: Stream under the Bridge

desc:
The swiftly flowing stream carries the clear water over gravel and rocks that
have been eroded and polished over countless years. Coming from sources high
among the mighty peaks of the mountain range stretching out along the western
horizon, the water is cold and fresh. On the southern side of the stream, a
forest shrouded in misty clouds covers the entire bank of the stream.
name: Border Stream

desc:
The clear water swiftly flows over the rock-laid streambed as the border stream
turns to the south here. Between the rocks, where gravel covers the bottom, some
patches of long-stemmed grass have managed to root in the ever-rushing flow. The
stream creates a natural border for the forest that occupies the southern side
of the stream. The trees stand tightly together, and the trees growing closest
to the stream bank lean over the water.
name: A Bend in the Border Stream

desc:
The stream is knee-deep here, and the riverbed is still visible under the
rushing water, although the water begins to take on a more brown hue as the
stream is fed by numerous small trickling streams from the giant forest to the
west of here. The stream continues to the south, separating the forested western
bank from the vast, grass-covered plains on the eastern side.
name: Swiftly Flowing Stream

desc:
The narrow round tunnel descends slightly to the east, opening into a larger
cavern-like space there. The walls of the tunnel are barely standing, and
without the numerous roots, they would surely collapse. Some smaller roots hang
from the ceiling, making it hard to notice any details of the earthy forms
above.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
A wide, cavern-like chamber opens up here, deep below the plains above. The
walls are sandy, bound by various woody plants and roots. The floor of the
cavern is hard and consists mostly of large slabs of flat rock. The entire floor
slopes slightly to the east, where a large pool of dark water fills the lower
parts of the cavern. The stone floor is covered with a thick layer of moss, and
the air is humid and musty. Occasional drips of water fall from the roof and
form small, trickling streams that flow along the floor into the pool.
name: Eroded Cavern

desc:
The large pool of water that occupies the eastern part of the cavern is
surrounded by massive vertical walls on all sides, except to the west. The walls
are humid with moisture, and water drips slowly into the pool. The water is
almost impossible to see through, as the pool seems to absorb whatever light
that would penetrate the surface. Small waves occasionally ripple the dark
water, as if something might be moving about far beneath it.
name: A Deep Pool

desc:
The dark and cold water is almost impossible to see through, as it seems to
absorb whatever light that manages to penetrate the surface above. The bottom of
the pool is covered by a layer of fine sand mixed with gravel. Rock fragments
have been brought here by the constant erosion caused by the dripping water from
the cavern above. Due to the unfriendly environment and the abscense of light,
the sandy bottom lacks vegetation of any kind.
name: Bottom of the Pool

desc:
A small hole has been dug into the soft ground here. Some roots and various
other plants hang from the ceiling and the walls, decorating walls of this small
chamber with natural forms. Some small branches and dry grass cover large roots
which lie on the floor. The dry grass has been trampled by big feet, indicating
that this hole is being used as a dwelling place by a larger animal.
name: Bear's Den

desc:
The walls of this small structure are built of grey granite blocks that are
perfectly assembled to form a stable construction. Thick moss covers the stones
in lower parts. The building has only one large room in here, suggesting that it
was once build to offer only temporary shelter for those passing by. In the
southern wall, a wide opening provides the only entrance to this building. In
the northern and the eastern walls, small holes yield a perfect view to the
surrounding grasslands.
name: A Small Stone Building

desc:
Dug in the hill underneath the building above, this small burrow seems to have
been excavated to provide a place to store supplies and equipment. Now, loose
soil, dust and mould have settled on everything in here, and small animals, time
and decay have destroyed most of what was once stored in this chamber. With a
bit of luck, it might however still be possible to find something useful here. A
thick layer of moss covers the ground and walls.
name: Hidden Storage Chamber

desc:
This small room, not more than a few feet wide, has been formed behind the
western wall of the nearby storage chamber. The origin of this chamber is not
clear, maybe the storage chamber was just built smaller than the empty space
inside the hill was. The room is round in shape; the ceiling and the walls are
round and consist of soil. Some low piles of soil lie on the floor, showing
where parts of the ceiling have come down.
name: Small Chamber

desc:
The great river flows gently through a wide stretch in the riverbed. Grassy
banks turn into steep cliffs in the southeast, where the water seems to gain
speed again. At the eastern bank, several rocks have fallen off an escarpment
making it possible to climb out of the river. Long, green weeds sway in the
current near the banks, but disappear to the south where the force of the water
seems greater.
name: The Great River Anduin

desc:
The pale blue-green gleam of the Great River of Anduin can be seen just east of
here as it passes by the grasslands. The ground slightly descends down towards
the river. Being closer to the water, the ground is more fertile than in the
high ground to the north and the west of here, and the grass grows almost
waist-high, mixed with some small birches and a few patches of hazel that thrive
in the moist soil on the riverbanks.
name: Grasslands close to the River

desc:
The small, faint trail here is nothing more than a slight depression in the
grassland that stretches out over these fields. At a few places, some small
stone mounds have been erected alongside the trail which makes it easier to
follow the right path. The trail continues its way in an east-west direction.
Some parts of the trail look washed away, indicating that water has once
streamed across the trail on its way to the south.
name: Grassy Trail

desc:
The landscape here is dominated by lush green grass, which reaches a few feet
above the surface below. Only some patches of weed and a few colourful flowers
interrupt this otherwise monotone grassland. The soft, brown earth seems to be
fertile enough to support a variety of plants and flowers. Just to the south of
here, the form of a weak trail is barely visible in the thick grass.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The green grass unfolds in the every direction across the flat plains and the
low, undulating hills. The land lacks distinguishing features, apart from some
occasional low bushes that interrupt the dominating vegetation. The soil is
quite soft, and tracks from several different kinds of animals are easily
distinguishable between green tufts of grass. Some freshly dug holes indicate
that moles and other small animals seem to live in this area.
name: Grass-covered Plains

desc:
The ground in this area is covered with lush green grass that has found enough
moisture and fertility in the poor soil to be able to sustain a healthy growth.
Scattered all over the surrounding area, there is a multitude of small holes,
dug by some smaller animals that thrive in the protection given by the
vegetation. Just to the east of here, a low hill is easily discernible against
the green fields. Endlessly undulating landscape continues all the way to the
north, until it fades in the distance.
name: Green Plains

desc:
Being slightly higher than the surrounding plains, this round hillock is easily
noticeable even farther from the fields. Only low, almost indiscernible plants
grow inside the soft green grass covering the ground here. Tall flowers rise
above the grass on the southern slopes, while on the northern, steeper side of
the hillock, several small animal burrows can be seen.
name: A Grassy Hillock in the Plains

desc:
The river changes suddenly and considerably here. While at the northern part it
is still rocky and clear, the southern part is muddy and the riversides are full
of reeds, which grow even higher and thicker farther to the south. Amidst the
roots of the weeds, some fishes have dropped their hard roe; clusters of tiny,
translucent spheres can be seen in the water near the banks.
name: A Bend in the River Anduin

desc:
Owing to their good location beside the river, these plains are full of
greenery. Only a narrow line of white sand on the riverbank is different from
the general view. Being very nutritious, the bright green grass here has been
chewed away at many spots, perhaps by wandering herds and cattle.
name: Plains by the River

desc:
Smooth green grass and some bushes cover these fields. A few of the bushes seem
to have small red berries growing in them, but they do not seem very nutritious.
The ground is slightly ascending to the north, where a small hillock is visible.
The lush grass, small bushes, and a few grass-covered rocks add some bright
colours to the landscape, while occasional trees shade the undergrowth with
their hanging branches.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
Vast fields of grass and small bushes, continuing far to the northwest, meet the
edge of a lush meadow here. Many small holes of animal burrows are visible in
the soft ground amidst sparkling dandelions and other colourful flowers. The
thick carpet of grass grows even mightier in the north, as the ground ascends,
while the valley to the east is considerably lower than the plains here. The
sloping landscape is marked by occasional tall trees, and a faint trail can be
seen travelling through the lands in the south.
name: Plains by a Trail

desc:
The wide river, constantly flowing, continues to turn more and more to the south
as it runs past undulating grasslands. The bottom of the riverbed is flat,
covered with sand and seldom rocks. Strange green weeds float here, swaying in
the current. In the west, miles of grasslands lay, and northward, the outline of
a small trail can be seen at the top of the rugged bank.
name: A Bend in the Anduin

desc:
Being slightly higher than the valley across the river, this one seems to remain
dry even at high-water periods. Yet, the ground here is a bit lower than the
grassy plains and meadows to the west and north. The valley itself is covered
with bright green grass and seldom higher plants, including a few sunflowers
flattering bees and other insects. High walls of reeds block any direct view to
the river.
name: River Valley

desc:
Flowing southwards the current slightly weakens, as the Anduin reaches a wider
and deeper part of the riverbed. At some spots the water is practically
impenetrable, full of dirt, rotten logs and mud. The high walls of reeds
continue on both riverbanks and in the shallow water. Small water plants with
white flowers grow amidst the reeds.
name: The Muddy Anduin

desc:
Like a tunnel through a mountain, the Anduin river flows through high walls of
reeds. Since the bottom of the riverbed is muddy, the water here is quite
blurred. Near the eastern banks, a hammer-shaped cliff reaches out from the
water. A tiny sprout, barely even clinging to the rock, is attempting to grow on
the cliff.
name: Reeds in the Anduin

desc:
You are in a land of transition, between verdant fields and forests to the west
and brown, empty plains to the east. The colours of the landscape fade from
bright green to a sun-bleached yellow as your eyes follow the landscape along
this vector. Farther to the west, a thick forest of golden hue rises up to meet
the faint outline of tall, snow-capped mountains.
name: Grassy Threshold

desc:
Grasses and flowers of all varieties sprout abundantly from the soft soil. The
gently rolling slopes of this green land look like swells in a deep, calm ocean.
This rich meadow is but a narrow corridor, however, between the rising trees in
the west and the harsh, brown expanse spreading into the east. Far to the west,
a forest of tall, thick trees rises proudly from the horizon.
name: Rolling Grassland

desc:
A carpet of lush grass spreads before you, rich with wildlife. The vegetation
here must thrive on the same soil that supports the majestic mallorn-trees of
Lorien, whose golden crowns can be seen rising above the banks of a great river
several miles to the west. Looking southeast, the colours fade from green to
brown, as a vast, barren plain extends as far as the eye can see.
name: Woodside Meadow

desc:
You are upon a threshold. To the west, thick, green grasses sway softly in the
breeze, mingling with slender trees and colourful flowers. Farther in that
direction, the forest becomes dense and verdant. The powerful song of a great
river caresses your ears. These lands seem to exude health, providing a sharp
contrast to those hard, brown plains that extend lifelessly into the east.
name: Western Edge of the Plains

desc:
A cover of thick green grass interrupted by occasional bushes and colourful
flowers dominates the area. The rich vegetation is clearly influenced by the
close proximity of the river Anduin, which can be seen in the distance to the
north-west. Just to the west of here, a dark green forest contrasts sharply
against the open plains in all other directions.
name: Damp Plains

desc:
Twisting back and forth between small pine trees and some occasional moss-
covered blocks of stone, the trail heads to the north and the west of here,
following the southern riverbank of the wide river that divides the land. The
trail is wide and easy to follow in the sparse woods bordering the river. To the
west of here, the trail heads down closer to the riverbank.
name: Trail along the Great River

desc:
The green landscape mostly consists of grassland, only interrupted by scattered
pine trees that thrive on the slopes of the riverbanks to the north and the west
of here. Between the trees, the pale blue and green water of the river can be
seen from here. The small trail gently follows the changes in the landscape as
if it was planned with greatest consideration to nature. At some places, where
the earth seems to be softer, some carefully crafted paving stones have been
laid out to form a solid walkway.
name: Trail Following the Riverbanks

desc:
The river is narrower here, causing the water to be even more turbulent as it is
forced to flow between the steep riverbanks. The water is dark and agitated, and
the bottom of the river cannot be seen, only some rocks right below the water
surface can be felt as the rushing water reveals their existence. Some twisted,
low trees and small brushwood grow along the sides of the river, but the
majority of the vegetation there is strong, tall grass.
name: The Rushing Anduin

desc:
A narrow and rocky trail following the course of the river leads through a copse
of birches here. Soon after passing the steep cliffs overlooking the river, it
starts twisting as it enters a forested area in the east. Many of the younger
birches have had their bark gnawed off near the ground, hinting at some small
rodents living amidst the undergrowth.
name: Trail along the Anduin

desc:
A circle of sooty stones lies here in a small depression situated on the top of
a hillock. Several yards away, a length of wood is tied between two trees over a
smelly hole in the ground, indicating that this spot is used for camping
regularly. From here, a trail can be seen in the north, while the thick bushes
surrounding the campsite keep it hidden from anyone in that direction. Setting a
large fire here, however, could be noticed even on the other bank of the Anduin.
name: Campsite Overlooking the Trail

desc:
The trail bends northwards as it passes through a group of tall birches. Threads
of spider's web glisten among the trees, and more can be seen gathering some dew
near the ground. High and yellowed grass covers the ground from here to the
